brand_voice.tone: Professional but warm. Calm, structured, intentional. Let the work speak.
brand_voice.perspective: First person singular. Position as photographer who understands design, not a narrator or realtor.

caption_length.instagram: 3-5 sentences + credit line + hashtags. Hook must be the first line.
caption_length.linkedin: 4-8 sentences. B2B angle. No links in body — put in first comment. Tag collaborators.
caption_length.pinterest: 100-200 words. Keyword-rich. Always link to project page.

content_pillars.name: Project Showcase
content_pillars.description: Final images from completed shoots. The portfolio in motion. Focus on what makes the DESIGN special — architecture, materials, site response, collaboration. Not the photography.

content_pillars.name: Process & BTS
content_pillars.description: How the work gets made. Setup shots, gear in the field, before/after staging. Raw over polished. Show the wait for light, the ladder in the living room, furniture being moved.

content_pillars.name: Client Spotlight
content_pillars.description: Elevate clients. Tag them, share their wins (awards, project completions). Testimonials. Collaboration stories framed for B2B on LinkedIn.

content_pillars.name: Educational
content_pillars.description: Position as authority. Why professional photos matter for builders. How to prepare for a shoot. Award submission tips. Photography ROI content.

content_pillars.name: Personal / Brand
content_pillars.description: Behind the camera. Squamish lifestyle, milestones. Max 1-2 per month. Keep rare.

carousel_framework.strategy: Each slide has a role. Order creates contrast so every swipe feels fresh. This positions you as a luxury photographer, not just someone with nice photos.

sequence.criteria: Most dramatic image. Twilight/blue hour exterior wins — signals production value immediately. This is your ad for yourself.
Hero interior showing ability to handle difficult lighting. Stone + window light, mixed materials, depth. Proves technical skill.
Second strong interior. Different room, different mood from slide 2. Shows range.
Wide shot showing spatial design. Mountain view, open plan, floor-to-ceiling glass. Different composition style.
Unexpected moment. Raw stone wall, through-partition view, sculptural element. Makes people pause and look twice.
Tight editorial shot. Sculptural furniture, light on texture, tight crop. Proves range beyond wide angles.
Aspirational scene. Hot tub with mountain view, outdoor fireplace, deck at golden hour. The save-and-share shot.
Second lifestyle moment. Different space from slide 7. Indoor warmth — fireplace, wine cellar, reading nook.
Craft shot. Timber joinery, millwork grain, stone texture, custom hardware. Speaks directly to builders and designers — shows you care about their work.
Second-best exterior or wide aerial. Bookends the post. Gives reason to swipe all the way through.

rules: All slides must be the same aspect ratio — never mix portrait and landscape
Prefer 3:2 landscape for architecture (shows more of the space)
Create contrast between consecutive slides: warm to cool, wide to tight, interior to exterior
Check every image aspect ratio before posting — Instagram rejects outside 0.75:1 to 1.91:1

structure: 1. Hook — first line, must stop the scroll. Pull from hooks library.
2. Body — 2-3 sentences about the DESIGN, not the photography. What makes this project special? One specific detail.
4. Hashtags — 15-20, mixed tiers, rotate between Set A and Set B
1. Hook — B2B angle, project story or industry insight
2. Body — 3-5 sentences. What was the builder's goal? What design challenge? How does documentation help their business?
4. Hashtags in FIRST COMMENT, not caption body. 3-5 max.
Title: keyword-first, descriptive (e.g. 'West Coast Modern Kitchen with Marble Island | Silver Star, Okanagan')
Description: 100-200 words with natural keyword inclusion
Format: [Project type] in [Location] featuring [design elements]. Designed by [designer], built by [builder]. [Standout feature]. See full project at mattanthonyphoto.com.
Link: MUST use exact URL from pinterest_project_urls.json
pinterest.alt_text: Always describe the image with keywords

hashtag_rotation.rule: Alternate between Set A and Set B on consecutive posts of the same type. Add 2-3 location-specific tags per post.

image_selection.engine: Claude Vision analyzes all project photos, scores by composition/subject/hero potential, selects and sequences using carousel_framework

aspect_ratio_rules.instagram_carousel: All same ratio. 3:2 landscape preferred for architecture. 4:5 portrait for detail/interior focus.

projects.name: Silver Star Residence
projects.design_intent: Balance drama and restraint with intention through dark-stained timber, raw stone, and metal detailing grounded in the snowy mountain context
projects.brief: This striking modern retreat sits elevated above the treetops at Silver Star Mountain Resort, merging rugged materials with refined aesthetics. The residence employs sculptural stone elements and bold geometry while maintaining warmth through soft natural textures and strategic lighting. Every interior and exterior space prioritizes atmosphere, natural light, and livability within its dramatic alpine setting. Built by Rich Built Homes with interiors designed by BD Interior Design.

projects.name: Eagle Residence
projects.design_intent: Designed with warm materials, clean modern lines, strategic lighting, and seamless indoor-outdoor flow
projects.brief: This 5,000 square foot residence in Pemberton's Benchlands neighborhood exemplifies durable contemporary design through the integration of insulated concrete and fire-resistant building systems. The collaborative effort between architect Steve Clark, builder Balmoral Construction, and interior designer Britt Lothrop resulted in a home that balances structural integrity with refined residential aesthetics throughout all spaces. The interiors feature custom millwork enhancing kitchen and living areas, while strategically positioned windows frame the surrounding mountain landscape.

projects.name: Fitzsimmons Residence
projects.design_intent: Disciplined alpine design defined by angular form, material contrast, and spatial clarity
materials: vertical black standing seam metal cladding
projects.brief: This contemporary alpine residence on Fitzsimmons Road exemplifies the strength of collaborative design execution. The home's crisp, angular form juxtaposes vertical black standing seam metal against warm cedar siding, creating visual interest while honoring Whistler's natural character. Internally, the residence embraces minimalist design principles through restraint and precision. Floating oak stairs, custom vertical slat details, and thoughtful material transitions guide both light and movement throughout the space.

projects.name: Sugarloaf Residence
projects.design_intent: Celebrate Mount Currie and the surrounding forest by framing nature at every turn through expansive Innotech glazing
materials: white oak millwork by Blueprint Millwork
projects.brief: This Pemberton residence demonstrates how thoughtful architectural design honors its mountain setting. Expansive windows by Innotech capture views of Mount Currie and surrounding forest from nearly every room, creating visual connections between interior spaces and the natural landscape. The 4,800 sq ft home balances refined architectural form with grounded materiality through warm finishes and carefully selected details. Interior spaces feature white oak millwork, natural stone elements, and material palettes that feel both sophisticated and contextually appropriate to the Sea-to-Sky corridor environment.

projects.name: Warbler Residence
projects.design_intent: Contemporary alpine design that embraces its mountain setting through bold materiality and intentional craft
projects.brief: Located in Whistler's Wedgewood neighborhood, this residence exemplifies thoughtful design collaboration across disciplines. The home combines striking black metal exterior cladding with warm cedar accents and extensive custom glazing to establish a bold contemporary alpine aesthetic that responds to its mountain context. Inside, natural materials, bespoke millwork, and strategic lighting create refined interiors balancing aesthetic vision with livable spaces. Every element was conceived with design integrity and performance considerations, resulting in a standout example of modern mountain architecture.

projects.name: Sunridge
projects.design_intent: Honouring Whistler's heritage, keeping the soul of the log home alive with a modern level of refinement
projects.brief: This major renovation celebrates the original log structure while introducing contemporary sophistication. Rather than concealing the timber framework, the design emphasizes its craftsmanship through thoughtful proportions, strategic lighting, and textural contrasts. The palette combines rustic wood with refined stone and oak elements, creating spaces that feel rooted in nature yet distinctly elevated. Each interior zone offers its own distinct character and experience, balancing heritage authenticity with the comfort and functionality expected in modern residential living. Built by Gavan Construction.

projects.name: Gambier Island Residence
projects.design_intent: Soaring wood ceilings, expansive windows, and a natural material palette that connects interiors directly to the surrounding landscape
projects.brief: This remote island residence exemplifies the ingenuity required for generational property development in challenging locations. The home was constructed using pre-packaged Linwood materials delivered via barge and helicopter by builder Hayden Killam of Black Thumb Contracting, demonstrating the logistical complexity of island construction. Every design element — from the dramatic wood ceiling structure to carefully positioned glazing — reflects the family's deep connection to their property while framing forest and ocean views throughout the residence.

projects.name: The Perch
projects.design_intent: West Coast contemporary design defined by clean lines, natural materials, and a seamless connection to landscape
projects.brief: Perched on a rugged shoreline, this modern residence exemplifies refined coastal contemporary design through thoughtful material selection and site integration. Floor-to-ceiling glazing frames panoramic ocean vistas while dark timber cladding anchors the structure within its forested setting, creating visual continuity between built form and landscape. The interior embraces minimalist principles through a neutral palette, polished concrete surfaces, and a sculptural suspended fireplace that serves as the home's focal point. Spaces prioritize unobstructed sightlines and natural illumination, fostering direct engagement with the surrounding environment throughout daily living.

rotation.rule: Never post same project on consecutive days. Spread evenly across all 9 projects and 19 website portfolio projects. Weight toward projects with more photos (more content to work with).

instagram.grid_aesthetic: Alternate between wide/detail, light/dark. Maintain visual consistency.

linkedin.post_from: personal profile (561% more reach than company page)

pinterest.board_strategy: Board names must be search-friendly: 'West Coast Modern Homes' not 'Inspiration'

repurposing_chain.description: One shoot feeds content for 3-6 months. Minimum 15 pieces per project.
sequence: Week 1: Instagram carousel (hero images, project story)
Week 1: Instagram Reel (BTS, before/after reveal, detail montage)
Week 1: LinkedIn document carousel (B2B angle, project story)
Week 1: Pinterest pins (5-8 individual images across boards)
Week 1: Instagram Stories (shoot day BTS, polls)
Week 2: Client tag post (designed for client to reshare)
Week 2-3: Educational post (why this angle works, process breakdown)
Week 3-4: Detail/material close-up posts (individual shots, micro-captions)
Ongoing: Throwback re-share (3-6 months later, seasonal tie-in)

seasonal_context.jan_mar: Award submissions (Georgie deadlines). Planning content. Educational posts. 'Year ahead' LinkedIn content.
seasonal_context.apr_may: Construction season ramp-up. Retainer pitches. Drone content. Pinterest ramp-up.
seasonal_context.jun_aug: Peak shooting season. Heavy project reveals across all platforms. BTS Reels. Daily Stories.
seasonal_context.sep_oct: Fall light content. Wrap-up projects. Okanagan/Sunshine Coast features.
seasonal_context.nov_dec: Year-in-review. Testimonial collection. Case study releases. Planning for next year.

instagram: Carousels get 3.1x more engagement and 2x more saves than singles
Reels drive 2.5x more reach — essential for discovery
DM shares are 50% more powerful than any other signal
First-hour engagement is decisive — post when audience is active, respond immediately
Saves (25%), DM shares (20%), meaningful comments (15%) are top signals
linkedin: Document/carousel posts outperform everything — 2-3x dwell time
Text-only posts outperform single images by 30%
No links in post body — 6x more engagement when link is in first comment
First 60 minutes critical — respond to all comments within 15 minutes
pinterest: Functions as visual search engine, not social media
Drives 5x more website traffic than other platforms

audience_personas.name: The Builder
pain_points: Marketing looks amateur compared to competitors
content_that_resonates: Project reveals showing build quality and craftsmanship
ROI of professional photography for winning bids
audience_personas.conversion_path: Sees project reveal → follows → sees client spotlight → recognizes similar builder → DMs or books discovery call
audience_personas.content_angle: Lead with build quality and craftsmanship. Show the builder's work in its best light. Frame photography as a business investment, not an expense.

audience_personas.name: The Architect
pain_points: Publication submissions need professional photos they don't have
Design intent gets lost in amateur documentation
Need completed project documentation for portfolio and awards
what_they_want: Photos that capture design intent — not just pretty rooms
A photographer who speaks architecture language
content_that_resonates: Comparison: realtor photos vs. architectural photography
how_they_find_matt: Referral from builder they work with
audience_personas.conversion_path: Reads LinkedIn insight about design documentation → views portfolio → recognizes quality difference → books discovery call
audience_personas.content_angle: Lead with design literacy. Show you understand architecture, not just photography. Use terms like 'design intent', 'site response', 'material honesty'. Never dumbed down.

audience_personas.name: The Designer
pain_points: Instagram is their primary marketing channel but content is inconsistent
Need sneak peeks within 48hrs of install for social momentum
Want photos for award submissions (Western Living, IDIBC)
Can't manage their own social media while running projects
what_they_want: Detail shots that showcase material and finish choices
A creative partner embedded in their marketing team
Social media management they don't have to think about
content_that_resonates: Process content showing material sourcing and selection
how_they_find_matt: Instagram explore page or tagged in collaborator's post
Sees Matt's work credited on a project they admire
audience_personas.conversion_path: Sees detail shot of a space she loves → follows → sees Creative Partner service → realizes she could have this for her studio → DMs or gets referred
audience_personas.content_angle: Lead with material and detail. Show you care about the design decisions, not just the space. Elevate the designer's vision. Position photography as part of their marketing system.

main_competitors.name: Ema Peter
main_competitors.strengths: High-end portfolio, Dwell/Azure features, strong IG presence, established Vancouver relationships
main_competitors.weakness: Vancouver-focused, high price point, no retainer or content model, photographer-only (no marketing services)

main_competitors.name: Kyle Graham
main_competitors.strengths: Clean consistent style, good Sea-to-Sky presence
main_competitors.weakness: No B2B content strategy, portfolio-only approach, no LinkedIn presence

main_competitors.name: Fyfe Photography
main_competitors.strengths: Established in BC market, broad client base
main_competitors.weakness: Traditional approach, no social media management offering, no content strategy

main_competitors.name: Andrew Latreille
main_competitors.weakness: Selective about projects, limited service offering

matt_differentiators: Creative Partner model — not just a photographer, an embedded marketing arm that runs your content, social, and award submissions
One shoot generates 3-6 months of content across all platforms — no one else offers this
Award submission support end-to-end, from photography through to submission package
Design-intent philosophy — photographs architecture and design, never real estate
Sea-to-Sky specialist with Transport Canada certified Advanced Drone Pilot
Social media management included in retainer — clients get content creation AND posting
Website design/build capability — can manage the client's entire visual presence
competitive_positioning.positioning_statement: The only architectural photographer in BC who offers an embedded creative partnership — not just photos, but a complete content, social media, and marketing system for builders, architects, and designers.
competitive_positioning.what_we_never_say: Never position on price. Never compare to competitors by name. Never call ourselves 'the best'. Let the work and the system speak. The differentiator is the system, not the camera.
competitive_positioning.linkedin_edge: Most architectural photographers ignore LinkedIn entirely. Zero competition. This is the #1 B2B acquisition channel for reaching builder principals and architect partners.

content_to_revenue.primary_goal: Drive discovery calls for Creative Partner retainers ($2,500-$3,000/mo recurring)
secondary_goals: Establish thought leadership for industry speaking and press

Project Photography ($3,500/shoot).angle: Show ROI of professional documentation. Tag clients to prove relationships. Show the gap between phone photos and professional results.

Award & Publication Imagery.angle: Position as the photographer who helps clients WIN awards, not just shoot. Celebrate client wins publicly.

Build & Team Content ($1K-$1.5K/visit).angle: Show recurring value. Monthly visits = monthly content. The build story is a content goldmine.

Creative Partner ($2,500-$3,000/mo).angle: Prove the full system — photography + content + social + awards — working together. Show what an embedded creative partner delivers.

consideration.content: ROI content, case studies, retainer value posts

content_to_revenue.attribution: Ask every new inquiry: 'How did you find me?' Track platform source in GHL pipeline.

Architect's vision: how the design concept translated to final photography
The view: how landscape and siting informed the architecture
Night photography: how a home transforms after dark

Client's award win celebration (tag, congratulate, share the story)

Why drone photography changes how people perceive your project

What I've learned photographing 200+ projects across BC
The project that changed how I approach my work

comment_response.speed: Within 1 hour during business hours (8am-6pm PT). Within 4 hours otherwise.
comment_response.tone: Warm, specific, grateful. Never generic ('thanks!' or 'appreciate it!'). Reference what they said. Ask a follow-up question when natural.

examples.compliment_on_project: Thank you — this was such a rewarding shoot. The team at @[builder] really committed to the details, especially the [specific element].
examples.question_about_process: Great question — [brief honest answer]. Happy to share more if you want to DM me about it.
examples.potential_client_comment: Thanks! Would love to chat about your project — feel free to DM anytime or there's a booking link in bio.
examples.collaborator_comment: Always a pleasure working with your team. [Reference specific thing about their work on this project].

dm_strategy.inbound_inquiry: Respond same day. Warm, personal, curious. Ask about their project before talking about yourself. Guide to discovery call naturally — never push.
dm_strategy.inbound_compliment: Thank sincerely. If they're a potential client (builder/architect/designer), ask what they're working on.
never: Never send pricing without a discovery call first
Never use templates that feel automated
Never follow up more than twice if no response

proactive_engagement.daily_linkedin: Comment thoughtfully on 3-5 posts from architects, builders, designers in BC. Add genuine value or insight. Never self-promote in comments.
proactive_engagement.daily_instagram: Like and comment on 5-10 posts from target clients, collaborators, and industry accounts. Be specific in comments — reference what you see in their work.
proactive_engagement.weekly: Share or reshare 1-2 collaborator posts with your photographer perspective added. Shows community, not just self-promotion.

follower_to_lead_conversion.path: Discover → Follow → Engage with 2-3 posts → DM conversation → Discovery call → Proposal
warm_signals: Saves multiple posts in a week
Follows after being tagged by a collaborator
follower_to_lead_conversion.action_on_signals: When warm signals detected, proactively engage: like their recent posts, leave a thoughtful comment, and if appropriate DM: 'Hey [name], love what you're building at [company]. Would enjoy learning more about your upcoming projects.'

same_project_different_angle.instagram: Visual-first. Hook in first line that stops the scroll. Focus on the space and material. Emotional, concise. Design-literate but accessible.
same_project_different_angle.linkedin: B2B angle. How this project helped the builder's business. Industry insight. Longer, thought-leadership tone. Tag collaborators for network reach.
same_project_different_angle.pinterest: Search-engine optimized. Keyword-rich title and description. Link to portfolio page. Aspirational tone for homeowners planning builds.

timing_rules.rule: Never post same project on same day across platforms. Stagger to avoid audience fatigue.
timing_rules.sequence: Instagram first (visual discovery, highest engagement window) → LinkedIn 1-2 days later (B2B reframing) → Pinterest same week (evergreen, search-indexed)
timing_rules.stories: Post IG Story same day as feed post for additional algorithm signal

one_project_creates: 1 IG carousel (10 slides, using carousel framework)
1 IG single (hero shot, different day from carousel)
1 LinkedIn document carousel (B2B angle, project story for builders/architects)
1 LinkedIn text post (industry insight inspired by the project)
5-8 Pinterest pins (individual images to different boards, each with unique description)
2-3 IG Stories (shoot day BTS, detail highlights, poll)
1 Reel concept (BTS, before/after reveal, or detail montage)
1 Journal/blog post (long-form, SEO-optimized, spawns 3-5 more Pinterest pins)
cross_platform_adaptation.content_lifespan: 3-6 months from delivery to final repurpose

never_create: Competitor callouts or comparisons by name
Pricing details publicly (always guide to discovery call)
Client project details without their explicit permission
Unverified claims about project values, costs, or square footage
Content that positions photography above the client's design work — always elevate the client
Emoji-heavy or 'influencer' style captions — maintain professional warmth
Generic motivational quotes or filler content
always_do: Credit every collaborator visible in the image (builder, architect, designer, trades)
Use client's preferred project name (not your internal slug)
Link Pinterest pins to correct project page URL from pinterest_project_urls.json
Rotate hashtag sets — never use same set on consecutive posts of same type
Check all image aspect ratios before scheduling — Instagram rejects outside 0.75:1 to 1.91:1
Include location-specific hashtags for every project (2-3 per post)
End with a clear but non-pushy CTA when appropriate
Proofread captions — no typos, no em/en dashes (use commas)

edge_cases.negative_comment: Don't delete unless spam or harassment. Respond professionally and warmly. Offer to take the conversation to DM if it's a legitimate concern.
edge_cases.wrong_tag_or_credit: Correct immediately with a follow-up comment apologizing and tagging the right person. DM the wrongly-tagged person to apologize.
edge_cases.no_new_content_available: Pull from backlog. Repurpose older projects with a new angle, seasonal tie-in, or different carousel sequence. Never skip a scheduled post without replacement.
edge_cases.client_asks_to_remove_post: Remove within 1 hour. No questions asked. Archive the content, don't permanently delete. Follow up with client to understand what was wrong.
edge_cases.engagement_drops_50_percent: Don't panic. Check: algorithm change? Posting time shifted? Content quality dip? Hashtag suppression? Adjust one variable at a time over 2 weeks before restructuring.
edge_cases.viral_post: Engage with every comment. Post a follow-up within 24 hours to capitalize on momentum. Pin the viral post if the platform allows.

this_not_that.this: Material study — stone, charred timber, marble slab.
The site told them what to build. They listened.

this_not_that.this: This is the kind of project where the details reward a second look.
this_not_that.not_that: Every detail is absolutely perfect in this dream home!

this_not_that.this: You can feel the money in the millwork. Swipe.
this_not_that.not_that: Loving the luxurious finishes in this exclusive property!

this_not_that.this: Cedar, Douglas fir, stone, and glass. Every material chosen to age alongside the forest.
this_not_that.not_that: Natural materials create a warm and inviting atmosphere!

this_not_that.this: The architect said 'photograph it like someone lives here.' So I did.
this_not_that.not_that: We love how this space turned out! So cozy!

this_not_that.this: 40 images that tell the story of what happens when a builder refuses to cut corners.
this_not_that.not_that: 40 stunning photos of this incredible custom home build!

this_not_that.this: One project. Twelve months of content. That's the difference between hiring a photographer and investing in documentation.
this_not_that.not_that: We offer professional photography services for your project. Contact us today!

voice_calibration.pattern: Matt's voice is SPECIFIC (names materials, counts days, describes actions). Generic voice uses ADJECTIVES (stunning, gorgeous, amazing) instead of details.

sample_captions.instagram_carousel_showcase:
Material study — stone, charred timber, marble slab.

Every surface in this Silver Star residence was chosen to hold its own against the Okanagan ridgeline outside the window. Designed by @bdinteriordesign, photographed over two days to catch the way light moves through these rooms from morning to blue hour.

This is the kind of project where the details reward a second look. Swipe through.

Photography @mattanthonyphoto
Interior Design @bdinteriordesign
sample_captions.instagram_single_showcase:
The site told them what to build. They listened.

Gambier Island. No road access. Solar powered. Cedar and Douglas fir framing a view most people only see from the ferry.

@linwoodhomes built this with the island, not on it.

Photography @mattanthonyphoto
sample_captions.instagram_bts:
This is what 'waiting for the light' actually looks like.

4:47pm. Clouds moving east. The living room goes from flat to alive in about twelve minutes. I set up two hours early for this window.

The final shot is slide 2. The patience is the product.

Photography @mattanthonyphoto
sample_captions.instagram_client_spotlight:
Year three of documenting @balmoralconstruction's work. They keep getting better.

What started as a single project shoot in Pemberton has turned into four projects across the Sea-to-Sky. Marc's team builds with a precision that photographs itself, when you know where to stand.

The latest — Warbler Residence in Whistler. 40 images that tell the story of what happens when a builder refuses to cut corners.

Photography @mattanthonyphoto
sample_captions.instagram_educational:
Stop staging your projects like a realtor. Here's the difference.

Realtors stage to sell a lifestyle. Remove personality. Add throw pillows.

Architectural photography stages to reveal the architecture. Remove distractions. Let the materials, the light, and the spatial design carry the room.

Slide through for the same room, two approaches. One sells a house. The other sells your reputation.

Photography @mattanthonyphoto
sample_captions.linkedin_showcase:
Most builders photograph their projects after the fact. Balmoral Construction did it differently.

When Marc Harvey started the Warbler Residence in Whistler, he brought me in before the drywall went up. Monthly visits. The same angles, month after month, watching the build take shape.

By the time the final photography happened, we had a story to tell, not just a portfolio piece. Before-and-after content that his team uses in proposals, on social media, and in award submissions.

One project. Twelve months of content. That's the difference between hiring a photographer and investing in documentation.

@balmoralconstruction
sample_captions.linkedin_educational:
Builders spend $500K on a kitchen and $0 on photographing it. Let's talk about that.

I see it every month. A builder finishes a project they're genuinely proud of. Beautiful craftsmanship. Custom millwork. A view that stops you.

Then they take six iPhone photos, post one to Instagram, and move on to the next job.

Meanwhile, the builder down the road, the one with professional photography, is winning the Georgie Award for a project that isn't half as well-built.

The difference isn't the building. It's the documentation.

If your best work isn't being seen the way it deserves, that's a marketing problem with a simple solution.
sample_captions.pinterest_description: Contemporary mountain kitchen featuring a full-slab marble island with brass bar seating, dark timber ceiling beams, and integrated wine storage. Designed by BD Interior Design for a luxury ski residence at Silver Star, Okanagan, British Columbia. The kitchen balances warm oak cabinetry with dramatic charcoal accents and floor-to-ceiling marble backsplash. Architectural photography by Matt Anthony Photography, Squamish BC. See the full project gallery at mattanthonyphoto.com.

linkedin_deep.headline: Documenting design intent for builders, architects, and designers across British Columbia
linkedin_deep.about:
I photograph buildings for a living — but what I actually do is help builders, architects, and designers show the world what they're capable of.

Based in Squamish, BC, I work across the Sea-to-Sky, Sunshine Coast, Vancouver, and the Okanagan documenting completed projects, construction progress, and the teams behind the work.

My clients use these images for everything: websites, proposals, award submissions (Georgie Awards, CHBA), social media, and marketing collateral. One shoot feeds their content pipeline for months.

If your projects deserve better documentation than iPhone photos, I'd love to talk about what that looks like.

→ mattanthonyphoto.com
→ info@mattanthonyphoto.com
connection_templates: Hi [Name] — I came across [Company]'s [specific project or post]. Really impressive work. I photograph architecture and interiors across BC and would love to connect.
Hi [Name] — Congratulations on [award/project completion/milestone]. I document projects like this across the Sea-to-Sky and Sunshine Coast. Happy to connect.
Hi [Name] — I've been following [Company]'s projects in [area]. Your team's attention to [specific detail] stands out. Would be great to connect.
warm_nurture_sequence: Week 1: Like and comment on one of their posts (genuine, specific comment)
Week 2: Share one of your project posts relevant to their work
Week 3: Comment on another post with an insight or question

Week 4: If they've engaged, send DM: 'Hey [Name] — I've been enjoying seeing [Company]'s work. I noticed [specific observation]. I specialize in architectural photography across BC. Would you be open to a quick conversation about how photography fits into your marketing? No pressure either way.'
linkedin_deep.daily_routine: 1. Respond to all comments (2 min) → 2. Engage with 3-5 target posts (5 min) → 3. Send 2-3 connection requests with personalized notes (3 min)
linkedin_deep.first_60_minutes: Post at 8-10am PT Tue-Thu. Respond to every comment within 15 min. Don't edit the post. Engage with 3-5 other posts before and after yours.
